summary: Completed testing of 'Ask Jessie' voice assistant bug fix. The microphone button is now visible and responsive on web. All 4 requested features verified. Backend API fully working (11/11 tests pass). Frontend text input fallback works correctly with AI response and TTS audio playback.

issues: Pulse animation on mic button makes Playwright report element as 'not stable' - requires force=True click. Minor UX consideration but not a bug.

jessie_page_loads.description: Jessie page loads correctly at /jessie with header 'Jessi', intro text 'Hi, I'm Jessi!', and 'Tap to talk or type below' instructions

mic_button_visible_clickable.description: Golden circle mic button is visible with data-testid='jessi-voice-btn'. Web-specific HTML <button> element renders correctly. Click requires force=True due to pulse animation.

text_input_fallback.description: Text input works - typing message and pressing Enter sends to /api/jessie/chat. User transcript and Jessie response displayed correctly. Voice playback indicator shows 'Jessi is speaking' with green speaker icon.

backend_chat_endpoint.description: /api/jessie/chat returns valid AI-generated response with session_id. Tested with include_voice: true - audio_base64 returned in mp3 format.

backend_history_endpoint.description: /api/jessie/history/{user_id} returns messages array with role and content

backend_tts_endpoint.description: /api/jessie/tts generates audio/mpeg response. Returns 400 for empty text or text > 4096 chars.

web_platform_detection.description: IS_WEB constant correctly detects web platform. Web-specific HTML button renders instead of TouchableOpacity.

critical_code_review_comments: expo-av deprecation warning in frontend logs - will be removed in SDK 54. Consider migrating to expo-audio and expo-video packages.
Microphone permission handling on web shows generic error message. Could improve UX with specific permission guidance.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Jessie voice assistant bug fix verified. The fix applied: 1) IS_WEB constant detects web platform, 2) Web-specific HTML <button> used instead of TouchableOpacity for mic button, 3) VAD modified to assume user is speaking on web since metering unavailable, 4) Web-specific audio recording options with webm format, 5) processVoice handles blob URLs on web. Backend uses EMERGENT_LLM_KEY for GPT-5.2 and OpenAI TTS with 'nova' voice. Text input is the recommended way to test Jessie in automated tests since microphone requires real browser permissions.

verification_notes.microphone_on_web: Cannot fully test in headless browser due to permission requirements. The HTML button implementation is correct and clickable - actual recording depends on user granting microphone permission in real browser.
verification_notes.fix_applied: expo-av metering doesn't work on web, so VAD now assumes user is speaking (hasSpokenRef = true) on web platform. Auto-stop timer after 10 seconds gives user time to speak.
